# Read Receipts

> Track and display which users have read each message, providing per-user read confirmation for messages in conversations

**Category:** Communication · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** read-receipts · messages · tracking · confirmation · delivery

## What this does

Track and display which users have read each message, providing per-user read confirmation for messages in conversations

Specifies 7 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **message_id** *(text, required)* — Message ID
- **room_id** *(text, required)* — Room ID
- **user_id** *(text, required)* — User ID
- **read_at** *(datetime, required)* — Read At
- **thread_id** *(text, optional)* — Thread ID
- **last_seen** *(datetime, optional)* — Last Seen
- **pinned** *(boolean, optional)* — Pinned
- **is_thread_reply** *(boolean, optional)* — Is Thread Reply

## What must be true

- **general:** Read receipts must be enabled by system configuration before any receipts are stored, A receipt is created for each message a user views, identified by user ID, message ID, and timestamp, Batch read: when a user opens a room, all visible unread messages up to their last-seen timestamp are marked as read, A message authored by the sender is automatically marked as read if the sender is the only participant in the room, Thread reads are tracked separately from main conversation reads, Once all subscribers have read messages up to a point, those messages are marked as globally read at the room level, Read receipts must not leak data: only the sender (and administrators) can see who read a specific message, Deleting a user's data must remove all their associated read receipts, Pinned messages retain their read receipt history even after pinning, Read receipts for a message are debounced per room to avoid write storms when many users open a room simultaneously

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Messages Marked As Read On Open** — when user opens or scrolls to a room and has unread messages; last_seen exists; read_receipts_enabled eq true, then Read receipts are stored for all unread messages up to the user's last-seen timestamp.
- **Thread Marked As Read** — when user reads messages inside a thread; thread_id exists; read_receipts_enabled eq true, then Read receipts are recorded for thread messages independently from the main room.
- **Message Auto Read By Sender** — when sender is the only participant in the room; subscriber_count eq 0; read_receipts_enabled eq true, then Message is automatically marked as read when the sender is alone in the room.
- **Room Last Message Read** — when all subscribers have seen the room's last message; All subscriptions have last-seen >= room's last message timestamp, then Room is marked as fully read; the last message shows as read by everyone.
- **Receipts Fetched For Message** — when user requests the list of readers for a specific message; message_id exists; read_receipts_enabled eq true, then List of users who have read the message is returned with their read timestamps.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Read Receipts Disabled** — when read_receipts_enabled eq false, then No read receipts are stored or returned; feature is disabled by system configuration. *(error: `READ_RECEIPT_FEATURE_DISABLED`)*
- **Receipt Not Found** — when user queries receipts for a message that has no receipt records; message_id exists, then Empty receipt list returned; no users have read the message yet. *(error: `READ_RECEIPT_NOT_FOUND`)*

## Errors it can return

- `READ_RECEIPT_FEATURE_DISABLED` — Message read receipts are not enabled on this server
- `READ_RECEIPT_NOT_FOUND` — No read receipts found for this message

## Connects to

- **user-presence** *(recommended)* — Last-seen timestamps from presence can be reused for batch read marking
- **channel-messaging** *(required)* — Messages must exist before read receipts can be tracked
- **direct-messaging** *(recommended)* — Read receipts are especially meaningful in direct conversations
